item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as the image seems to comes from here with no indication it has been released under Creative Commons. As such, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting thought. Do have to point out that this might get noticed (creating it's own problems) but there is always slipping it in as an addendum to a larger measure. Also possible that it isn't actually bound by traffic laws, and it just follows them because it makes it easier to blend in most of the time, but still might be worth a shot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
nor has it broken any traffic laws.

I think the Foundation should have an addition made to the traffic laws: "Phasing through other vehicles is illegal." That should seriously reduce the number of information breaches that occur.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this one! The radio logs gave me goosebumps a couple times! Great work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this!!! Scary, for sure. It's chilling and I think it works perfectly. +1

item.title: Nitpicky, but...
item.content:encoded: I feel like this shouldn't have public knowledge "contained". Kinda seems a strange thing to contain knowledge. Disinformation efforts? Maybe "curtailed" instead?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't say I'm a fan— it doesn't really do anything to break the mold of a random item generator.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
[Note that this warehouse is overlooking a cliff, this is geographically impossible]

This should have a conjunction, or "as" or something. I would slip in a fix, but there are baby ways to do it and I'm not sure which one you'd like.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Minor grammar nitpick- in the report for object #13 (The big eye) shouldn't it be "agents' large intestines" rather than "agent's" since it's referring to both of them?

But regardless of that, I like it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Those classes are for how difficult to contain it is, not how dangerous it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Should this really be Keter? Yes, it's uncontained, but plenty of uncontained skips are Euclid, and I don't think this is dangerous enough to be considered Keter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Right. So, what do we got here. ;>
Here are the things that bother me (and some that don't!):

+ show block
– hide block

Public knowledge of SCP-2590's existence is to be contained by confiscation of video footage and photographs, and administration of Class-A amnestics to eyewitnesses.

I don't get this. I know that taking pictures and videos of everything and anything is a serious health concern now ;>, but… why would people take pictures and videos of a truck driving around in the streets of Birmingham, AL? I would understand if there was NO driver in the cabin - but there is a humanoid shadow thing, and by the time the truck drives past the sickest picture- or video-taker and the poor picture-addict realizes it's only a shadow, it's too late to take a picture. I don't think you need this sentence at all.
OTOH, you could mention that the 'pursuing operatives' (I like that!!) are to prevent any pedestrian from getting too close to the driver's cabin…?

In the event SCP-2590 comes to a stop,

As it is written now, I understand 'any kind of stop', which is why I asked you if the truck obeyed traffic laws. How about 'In the event SCP-2590 comes to a stop not dictated by traffic laws'?

It lacks a license plate on either the front or rear bumper.

As I told you in my first post, vehicles, esp. trucks, are highly regulated and controlled in Western countries. A truck without license plates would be stopped by police so often it would probably run 200 metres per day! Personally, I think you should have it have license plates, bogus ones for instance, but ones that look acceptable at first sight. It would be an echo of the shadow driver: the SCP 'saying' "I'm anomalous, but I want to be left in peace doing my anomalous stuff all day long". What do you think?

The model's manufacturer, Navistar International, claims to have no knowledge of ever producing a vehicle like SCP-2590.

I don't think you can write this. The Foundation cannot tell Navistar that the truck is driving around without a real driver and popping objects/people at its back; so Navistar does not know it's anomalous. Ergo, as far as Navistar is concerned, they must have produced hundreds or thousands of vehicles like SCP-2590.
How about the Foundation being able to read the Vehicle Identification Number for the tractor or trailer (not sure it's easily read though - can't imagine Foundation agents able to open the hood and look for it…!), and going to Navistar with it? Then Navistar can check and get back with: 'Nope. That baby ain't ours'.

SCP-2590-1's significance to the function of SCP-2590 is currently unknown, however, it has been proven to have an amnestic-like effect on humans approaching it on foot.

I remember that you mentioned a policeman somewhere, but apparently you got rid of it since. How about just adding:
to have an amnestic-like effect on humans (including on-duty policemen) approaching it on foot.
to cover that possibility?
(and what happens when a police car stops by at a traffic light and the policeman goes 'Hey, you, up there!!!"?? - Naah, no worry, I'm joking!!!! Still… ;>)

During the time the door is open, the trailer will be occupied by a single object. The object within the trailer is different each time the door closes and opens again. Each instance is unique, and no object has ever been observed twice.

Sentences 2 and 3 say the same thing, IMO. You could easily delete one or the other - preferably sentence 2, I think.

List of objects:
What bothers me here, and made me believe you don't know enough about your skip, is that I cannot see the logic of those objects. Any logic, including WTF logic! Is there one? ;>
I would understand first a few random objects, then people (distressed human male, panicked child) then Foundation people. But what about apple, sphere, lightbulb, eyeball, especially if the ultimate goal of the skip is to piss off the Foundation, which is how I understand your article? (Okay, the eyeball did piss them off, in a different way…!)

Addendum A:
This looks strange, because it refers to 'object' n° 16 which is hidden in the collapsible, while the addendum is not. Would be a good idea to place it within the list, as an afterword. What say you?
This also applies to Addendum B, BTW.

Incident 3 - general comment:
I cannot, for the life of me, find any sort of link, WTF or otherwise, between what the reader knows of the skip so far and this incident. I'm completely lost. I would very much like to know other people's opinions about this! (and the rest of my comments, in fact…)

Update, the skip has started moving away from us

This means that the truck was waiting for Foundation agents to turn up, right? Good touch!

Parchment & message:
?????! Good touch on the trailer door opening, but this is a let-down ;>! And doesn't make much sense to me… (I'm actually starting to feel very stupid here…)

[Note that this warehouse is overlooking a cliff, this is geographically impossible]
0419 Hours MTFC Gamma-133: "Alpha Squad, be advised you are continuing into a confirmed spatial anomaly. That tunnel shouldn't be there.

Rather than telling about the tunnel, why don't you show it, by including it into the MTFC's reply - which otherwise is not very clear for the squad… Something like:
The warehouse is overlooking a cliff - that tunnel shouldn't be there.
What say you?


***
OK. End of endless nitpicking!

In reply to Proxymoron's comment:

I really like SCPs that leave you with a total "what the fuck" experience, and this one definitely delivers.

I'm not there yet. For me, SCP-1193 delivers, because it's so far removed from our reality that nothing makes sense and nothing can make sense, and it's fine that way. Here, the anomaly is still too close to reality (vehicle, city streets, traffic laws, etc.) - the balance between 'making sense' and 'WTF' is much more difficult to attain.

As a conclusion:
Yeah - I do realize that you have been gaining steady upvotes since your latest edits, so perhaps I should just shut my damn big mouth and let you bask in your glory…! ;-D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the comparison with George Lucas ;>

Trust me, no matter how it may seem that I don't, I know EXACTLY what's going on with this thing.

All right. Then allow me to ask a question: why do we go from a Kit-Kat bar to a red apple to O5-██?

I'll do a line-by-line of the other stuff that bothers me, but a bit later. Bear with me!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I often encounter similar experiences when writing my own stuff, so you're not alone here.

I actually really like what you've done with the SCP as a whole. It started off meh, then after the initial overhaul it was good, and now with the added incident report, I think it's a solid SCP. I really like SCPs that leave you with a total "what the fuck" experience, and this one definitely delivers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm sorry you feel that way.

+ block
- block

Trust me, no matter how it may seem that I don't, I know EXACTLY what's going on with this thing. I have the entire story already put together. My struggle is when I try to get it out, I screw it up and reveal too much or too little. That's the feedback that I've been given on this skip and the other one I've written. As for the other points you've made:

It's irrelevant if it gets stopped 10 times a day, as the officers forget all about it due to 2590-1's effect. It also doesn't run red lights, it specifically says the vehicle has not been seen breaking traffic laws.
The truck passing through tangible matter selectively is the reason they cannot simply stick it in a garage and bolt it to the ground. How do you catch a vehicle that will literally just ghost right through any roadblock you set up? I'll clear that up in the article, but that's the reasoning behind it, and why it's classified Keter.
The contaimnent procedures say 'don't approach 2590-1' because of its amnestic effect. Again, every time I try to improve the article I make something else worse. I'm gonna go in and fix that part so it is clear.

I know this SCP is not going to be for everyone, and I know a lot of that is due to me jumbling up the format with every edit I make. I feel like George Lucas, I know I've got a good thing going here, but every time I put it into words I look like a babbling fool. I am trying though, make no mistake about it. I'm not just throwing words on the page to see what sticks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My apologies, Multiscoop, couldn't reply to your PM yesterday and was busy this morning!

<scratches head> Okay. I'm sorry to say that I like it less with the latest additions.

I still think the starting idea is interesting, but… it seems to me to be going every which way, with no logic or coherence.

+ show block
– hide block

I can only agree with WJS above:

do you know what the story behind the truck is?

… because I have a feeling that you don't. You've been adding anomalous elements that, IMO, have nothing to do with what you had before, = the strange objects appearing in the trailer.
Oh, BTW: when I first read the skip, I mixed up 'trailer' and 'tractor' (if that is the right word - my dictionary doesn't seem knowledgeable about big trucks!), so I pictured the objects and the steel slab appearing in front, in the driver's cabin. It actually doesn't change much…! Though it does beg the question: has anyone ever gone to the front of the truck to have a look/chat at the shadow driver? What happened? The containment procedures do mention it shoudn't be done - why?

I keep thinking that this should get a rewrite, tightening up the description/narrative/containment measures, and trimming into the list of objects. Make it an anomaly that, shortly after its discovery by the Foundation, makes annoying the Foundation its primary, if not sole, goal.
As it is now, it's got random objects, unknown people, Foundation people, a lot of blood, a space-time anomaly in a tunnel, message on a parchment… Plus automatic amnestic treatment to policemen who stop it (I remind you it has no license plates, so it must be stopped about ten times a day?), stops only when it pleases and not at stop signs or red traffic lights (what happens then??), gets filmed and photographed in the streets (WHY??), and is denied paternity by its manufacturing company.
Oh, and also this:

SCP-2590 selectively interacts with tangible objects, allowing it to 'pass through' physical matter.

Mentioned once, and never developped.


No. I'm sorry, but it doesn't appeal to me at all now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel the article has been strengthening over the past two revisions. Just to check, do you know what the story behind the truck is? As long as you do, it's easy to figure out what pieces you can reveal and it's easier for the pieces to maintain cohesion.

Also, I found this and just had to share.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is certainly interesting, as others have said. You've got a particular feeling going on here, but I can't make heads or tails of any significance to any of the stops. A stronger story thread would definitely help this along.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My take on it
Its all about the balancing act with this sort of article

On 04/12/2011 at approximately 3:15 AM, SCP-2590 travelled to the location of an abandoned warehouse…
[…]
…investigation. The following is a log of the first communications with Alpha Squad Leader:

So far, so good. This is well trod territory for the Foundation, but has the appropriate sort of "just another day at the foundation" vibe to go with it. Some tightening wouldn't hurt- "SCP-2590 was found {idling/parked/doing donuts?}", for example- and you might break up the information and order of events a little.
Start with an initial report log

Alpha Squad Leader: "SCP-2590 is slowly driving toward what looks like a descent to an underground tunnel, straight ahead from the entrance to the warehouse…"

and have them instructed to follow. Think swat team or special forces- they get trained in standard procedures to follow for scenarios like "an anomaly enters an area of suspected atypical space-time", which would probably include some sort of "should we pursue?" option, since some of these things are one way. The "have two stay behind and the rest follow" does seem like a reasonable way to go from there, however.

That would also let you focus the "re-establishing contact" log on what happened after they lost contact, rather than having to backtrack with information that the foundation should have already known (what could they see upon arrival at the warehouse).

The last two things I'd suggest ditching entirely ("+ List of SCP-2590 objects, post-Incident 2590-3" and the paragraph that starts "SCP-2590 came to the Foundation's notice when…")

There's only one in the post incident list that didn't tip over into creepypasta territory for me:

#6 Written in [DATA EXPUNGED] on parchment. I'm just delivering the message.

The others come across as rather campy, but have that described as written in black on a white sheet of paper after it tried to get them to follow it to wherever it went… that's an ending there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You have potential for a good WTF???? article here, but as already stated, and even with the latest edits, you don't go far enough.

+ show block
– hide block

For instance, I like Scorpion451's ideas: you have already edited in a tracking beacon, you should consider applying the rest.
A few other ideas:
Does the truck run perpetually without gas? If not, does it fill up only at unmanned gas stations? But then, does the shadow driver pay with a credit card? If he does, can said credit card be traced back to someone/something/somewhere?
Does the truck always respect the highway code? (uh, not sure this is the right translation…!) Has it never been stopped by police?
-> in general, vehicles (especially trucks) are tightly regulated and controlled in Western countries - doesn't seem too believable to have an anomalous one just strolling around happily…!
Is the trailer empty? One can wonder why whoever launched this anomaly decided to bother with a useless trailer!
Is there a way to read the individual references of either the tractor of the trailer, and check their official history with the manufacturer?
Apart from trying in every way to annoy the Foundation, has it ever done something harmful, or dangerous?
Do you, the author, know what's going on?
If you do: do try to give us, the readers, something a bit more thrilling than just a truck going around Birmingham, AL, and occasionally producing a red apple or gallons of blood?
If you don't: I suggest you find out! ;>

On another note: in the list of objects, instead of repeating every time that communication is not possible, you should perhaps mention it once and for all in a foreword?

A line-by-line would help tighten up and 'clean' your text (some unclear sentences, typos, etc) but I think you should consider the plot first. If you take this back to sandbox, PM me, I can help!

No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks a bunch! Your feedback is truly valuable to me :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Made a bunch of edits, hopefully for good this time. I changed the end result as well.

Thanks for all the feedback, folks. I really do appreciate it.

EDIT: My sandbox has a bunch of additions I have made to the plot, hopefully for better. I would like feedback before adding them to the main article, so please, let me know what you think.
Link: http://scpsandbox2.wikidot.com/multiscoop

EDIT EDIT: Made changes to the sandbox that Scorpion451 so kindly helped with, and put them into the article. They are designed to give it more story. Also fixed a bunch of unclear sentences, and added the manufacturer bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2590 is a semi-trailer truck of an unknown make
Going by the pic, it's a late-model International ProStar® tractor in DayCab configuration.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with the popular sentiment that this suffers from a weak and incoherent ending that doesn't work well with what comes beforehand. It's competently written and there's promise to the idea, but the ending is too simplistic to sell the story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Technically competent, but goes absolutely nowhere and otherwise has no payoff. Feels like a cobbled together collection of parts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The idea you have here is definitely executed well and kept me interested, but the tone and grammar could use some touching up (especially in the object logs).

The revision you did changed my initial neutral vote to a tentative upvote though.

EDIT: The newest revisions you've done have confirmed a solid upvote from me. Well done!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I still find that assuming the intervals are random isn't actually supported here. I think you want "unpredictable intervals" or "based on currently unknown variables" or something. AFAICT, there's no reason that the Foundation would have to assume a statistically random process as more likely than one based on variables that aren't known to them. It could be random, but it also could not be, and given that very few things in reality are actually random, it probably isn't.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the idea behind this SCP. Nevertheless the second incident - in my eyes - does not fit into the story. It seems a bit weak in contrast to the other findings.

Therefore I vote neutral.

Edit: after the changes it is an upvote for me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Agree that the ending isn't gelling with the rest.
To me it feels like a non-ending of the weak sort; you build it up with the interesting logs and hints of more going on… and then it kills two agents and is never seen again. That sort of "so we still have no clue what's going on here" ending can work, but it's very hard to pull off. Kudos if you can make it happen, but sometimes a little bit of explanation can go a long way. Right now you're ending on a weak answer: "it doesn't like the Foundation", when you could be ending on a strong question ("what does this thing really want, and why?") or even following it down the rabbit hole into higher planes of weirdness.

One of the Foundation's specialties is applying scientific curiosity to the anomalous, trying to figure it out:
Can they attach a tracking beacon to it to figure out where it goes when it isn't active?
Maybe weld it in place, so it's considered part of the vehicle and won't get left behind?
If they figure out where it goes, what do they find there? Further weirdness? An anomalous warehouse? A normal parking lot?
Can they put a tire boot on it, or remove its tires so it cannot drive off, or tow it away?
Can they get a better look at the driver by flying a drone-mounted camera directly up to the window?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Your article, you edit it however you want!

But that doesn't mean editing what you consider is wrong by partial feedback will be enough to fix all this. However, what I'm reading is not technically bad1.

Footnotes
1. I'm reading this with half an eye due to a mild sleep deprivation, so I'll re read it tomorrow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are some promising elements to this - the build up of items in the truck is decent, if a little slow - but I don't think the current ending works. "Murder then disappearance" seems a little too pat for something that could have gone in almost any direction - I was hoping it was merely another step towards a more complex resolution, but you could live without it entirely.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Your article, you edit it however you want!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Would it be okay if I cleared those parts up? Or am I not allowed to do that once it's posted to the main site?

Edit: I want to thank the user GramTax for helping me a ton with this article through the IRC chat. Posting here because I know double-posting is frowned upon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If they have to resort to less conventional means of containment (trailing it), it should have been Keter from the get-go.

That aside, I find this current version to be weaker than the draft I've seen some days back. 2590-2 makes little sense; it is a slab containing the blood of the people it killed by crushing them. I don't see any reason why it should be a sub-designation.

I believe that SCP-2590 can be safely assumed to be self-aware.

Given that you have highlighted that it is driven by 2590-1, it can be assumed from the get-go that a sentient force (the driver or 2590-1) is controlling all this.
